ابنيلي تطبيق كامل اسمه "مندوبك" - منصة دليفري زي أوبر. استخدم "القلب النووي Trinity Stack" ونفذ كل القواعد الجاية بالحرف:

=== فكرة التطبيق بالتفصيل ===
1. نوعين يوزر: عميل + مندوب + ادمن
2. المندوب: يسجل باسمه، رقم موبايل، عنوان، صورة البطاقة وش وضهر. رقم الموبايل Unique مينفعش يتكرر. ليه صفحة بروفايل ونسبة تقييم.
3. العميل: يطلب أوردر. الأوردر نوعين: أ. مشتريات من مكان واحد أو أكتر ب. توصيل من عنوان A لـ عنوان B. العميل ميدفعش فلوس، بيدفع تمن التوصيل بس.
4. التسعير: أنا كـ ادمن بحدد سعر الكيلو/المنطقة في لوحة تحكم. أول ما العميل يدخل العناوين، التطبيق يحسب سعر التوصيل أوتوماتيك قبل ما يأكد الأوردر عشان المندوب مينصبش.
5. صفحة العروض/الإعلانات: دي صفحة الهوم. أنا كـ ادمن بنزل عروض مطاعم/محلات بمقابل مادي. لو العميل ضغط على عرض، التطبيق يسجله أوردر جاهز بالعنوان والمنتج ويقوله "تأكد الطلب؟".
6. لوحة المناديب: كل المناديب شايفة الأوردرات الجديدة Live. أول مندوب يدوس "قبول" الأوردر يتحجز باسمه ويختفي من عند الباقي في أقل من 0.1 ثانية. ممنوع مندوبين ياخدوا نفس الأوردر.
7. الأمان: ممنوع مندوب يسجل برقمين مختلفين. رقم الموبايل + صورة البطاقة = بصمة واحدة.

=== التكنولوجيا - القلب النووي ===
1. Frontend: Astro + TypeScript + Tailwind + Shadcn UI على Cloudflare Pages
2. Backend: Cloudflare Workers + Hono.js للـ API
3. Auth: Clerk - تسجيل بجوجل/رقم/OTP + 2FA + منع رقم موبايل مكرر
4. Database: Supabase Postgres + Prisma ORM
5. Cache L1: Cloudflare KV عشان السباق على الأوردرات
6. Storage: Cloudflare R2 لصور البطايق والمنتجات
7. Realtime: Supabase Realtime عشان الأوردر يختفي لحظياً من عند كل المناديب

=== القواعد الذهبية - دي اللي هتخليه أسطوري ===
1. منع السباق على الأوردر Race Condition: استخدم "Atomic Transaction" في Supabase + Cloudflare KV Lock. الكود: لما مندوب يدوس قبول، اعمل SELECT FOR UPDATE. لو الأوردر status=pending غيره لـ accepted وخزن id المندوب. لو status اتغير خلاص ارجعله "الأوردر اتحجز". ده لازم يكون في Transaction واحدة.

2. الأمان 100% RLS: 
   - جدول orders: اليوزر يشوف الأوردر بتاعه بس. المندوب يشوف الأوردرات اللي status=pending + الأوردرات بتاعته بس.
   - جدول couriers: المندوب يشوف بروفايله بس. الادمن يشوف الكل.
   - صور البطايق في R2: اللينك يكون Signed URL صالح 5 دقايق بس.

3. منع تكرار المندوب: في Clerk اعمل Action يمنع تسجيل نفس رقم الموبايل مرتين. وفي Supabase اعمل Unique Index على رقم الموبايل في جدول couriers.

4. السرعة 100%: أي طلب "جيب الأوردرات الجديدة" يتكاش في Cloudflare KV 3 ثواني بس. عشان المناديب تشوف الأوردر لحظي بس السيرفر ميقعش.

5. حساب السعر: اعمل جدول admin_pricing فيه [المنطقة, سعر_الكيلو, حد_أدنى]. API يحسب المسافة بين النقطتين بـ Haversine formula ويضرب × السعر + الحد الأدنى. النتيجة تظهر للعميل قبل الدفع.

6. الكود نضيف: فولدرات /api/orders /api/couriers /api/admin /components /lib/auth /lib/pricing

=== المطلوب منك دلوقتي ===
1. Prisma Schema كامل: جداول users, couriers, customers, orders, order_items, addresses, admin_pricing, ads_offers
2. RLS Policies كاملة SQL جاهزة للنسخ في Supabase
3. API Endpoints Hono: POST /orders, GET /orders/pending, POST /orders/:id/accept, POST /upload-id, GET /pricing/calculate
4. كود Cloudflare KV Lock للـ accept order عشان السباق
5. كود رفع صورتين البطاقة على R2 + يخزن اللينك
6. صفحة هوم فيها قسم العروض + صفحة طلب أوردر + لوحة المندوب Live
7. ملف .env.example بكل الـ Keys
8. ملف wrangler.toml

ملحوظة: التطبيق لازم يستحمل 500 مندوب فاتحين صفحة الأوردرات في نفس الثانية ومحدش يعرف يسرق أوردر من التاني.

# ============================================================
# مندوبك - Mandoubak Delivery Platform
# .env.example - انسخ الملف ده لـ .env وحط فيه القيم الحقيقية
# ============================================================

# === Supabase ===
SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxxxxxxxxxx.supabase.co

R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# === App Config ===
NODE_ENV=development

API_URL=http://localhost:8787

// =============================================================
// مندوبك - Prisma Schema
// =============================================================

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider  = "postgresql"
  url       = env("DATABASE_URL")
  directUrl = env("DIRECT_URL")
}

// =====================
// Users (Clerk-synced)
// =====================
model User {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  clerkId     String   @unique @map("clerk_id")
  email       String?  @unique
  phone       String?  @unique
  role        UserRole @default(CLIENT)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  courier     Courier?
  customer    Customer?
  orders      Order[]  @relation("ClientOrders")

  @@map("users")
}

enum UserRole {
  CLIENT
  COURIER
  ADMIN
}

// =====================
// Couriers (المناديب)
// =====================
model Courier {
  id              String        @id @default(cuid())
  userId          String        @unique @map("user_id")
  name            String
  phone           String        @unique
  address         String
  idFrontImageUrl String        @map("id_front_image_url")
  idBackImageUrl  String        @map("id_back_image_url")
  status          CourierStatus @default(PENDING_REVIEW)
  rating          Float         @default(0.0)
  totalDeliveries Int           @default(0) @map("total_deliveries")
  isOnline        Boolean       @default(false) @map("is_online")
  createdAt       DateTime      @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt       DateTime      @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  user            User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  orders          Order[]       @relation("CourierOrders")
  ratings         CourierRating[]

  @@map("couriers")
}

enum CourierStatus {
  PENDING_REVIEW
  APPROVED
  REJECTED
  SUSPENDED
}

// =====================
// Customers (العملاء)
// =====================
model Customer {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @unique @map("user_id")
  name      String
  phone     String   @unique
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  addresses Address[]
  orders    Order[]

  @@map("customers")
}

// =====================
// Addresses (العناوين)
// =====================
model Address {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  customerId String   @map("customer_id")
  label      String
  street     String
  city       String
  district   String?
  latitude   Float
  longitude  Float
  isDefault  Boolean  @default(false) @map("is_default")
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  customer   Customer @relation(fields: [customerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  ordersFrom Order[]  @relation("PickupAddress")
  ordersTo   Order[]  @relation("DeliveryAddress")

  @@map("addresses")
}

// =====================
// Orders (الأوردرات)
// =====================
model Order {
  id              String      @id @default(cuid())
  orderNumber     String      @unique @map("order_number")
  clientId        String      @map("client_id")
  courierId       String?     @map("courier_id")
  customerId      String?     @map("customer_id")
  type            OrderType
  status          OrderStatus @default(PENDING)
  pickupAddressId String?     @map("pickup_address_id")
  deliveryAddressId String?   @map("delivery_address_id")
  pickupDetails   String?     @map("pickup_details")
  deliveryDetails String?     @map("delivery_details")
  pickupLat       Float?      @map("pickup_lat")
  pickupLng       Float?      @map("pickup_lng")
  deliveryLat     Float?      @map("delivery_lat")
  deliveryLng     Float?      @map("delivery_lng")
  distanceKm      Float?      @map("distance_km")
  deliveryFee     Float       @map("delivery_fee")
  notes           String?
  acceptedAt      DateTime?   @map("accepted_at")
  completedAt     DateTime?   @map("completed_at")
  cancelledAt     DateTime?   @map("cancelled_at")
  createdAt       DateTime    @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt       DateTime    @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  client          User        @relation("ClientOrders", fields: [clientId], references: [id])
  courier         Courier?    @relation("CourierOrders", fields: [courierId], references: [id])
  customer        Customer?   @relation(fields: [customerId], references: [id])
  pickupAddress   Address?    @relation("PickupAddress", fields: [pickupAddressId], references: [id])
  deliveryAddress Address?    @relation("DeliveryAddress", fields: [deliveryAddressId], references: [id])
  items           OrderItem[]
  adOffer         AdOffer?    @relation(fields: [adOfferId], references: [id])
  adOfferId       String?     @map("ad_offer_id")

  @@map("orders")
}

enum OrderType {
  SHOPPING     // مشتريات
  DELIVERY     // توصيل من A لـ B
}

enum OrderStatus {
  PENDING      // منتظر مندوب
  ACCEPTED     // اتحجز من مندوب
  PICKED_UP    // المندوب اخده
  DELIVERED    // وصل
  CANCELLED    // اتلغى
}

// =====================
// Order Items (منتجات الأوردر)
// =====================
model OrderItem {
  id          String  @id @default(cuid())
  orderId     String  @map("order_id")
  name        String
  description String?
  quantity    Int     @default(1)
  price       Float?
  shopName    String? @map("shop_name")
  shopAddress String? @map("shop_address")
  imageUrl    String? @map("image_url")

  order       Order   @relation(fields: [orderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("order_items")
}

// =====================
// Admin Pricing (التسعير)
// =====================
model AdminPricing {
  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
  zone           String   @unique
  pricePerKm     Float    @map("price_per_km")
  minimumFee     Float    @map("minimum_fee")
  maximumFee     Float?   @map("maximum_fee")
  isActive       Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  @@map("admin_pricing")
}

// =====================
// Ads & Offers (العروض)
// =====================
model AdOffer {
  id           String    @id @default(cuid())
  title        String
  description  String?
  imageUrl     String    @map("image_url")
  shopName     String    @map("shop_name")
  shopAddress  String    @map("shop_address")
  shopLat      Float     @map("shop_lat")
  shopLng      Float     @map("shop_lng")
  productName  String    @map("product_name")
  productPrice Float?    @map("product_price")
  isActive     Boolean   @default(true) @map("is_active")
  clickCount   Int       @default(0) @map("click_count")
  startDate    DateTime  @map("start_date")
  endDate      DateTime  @map("end_date")
  createdAt    DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime  @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  orders       Order[]

  @@map("ad_offers")
}

// =====================
// Courier Ratings (التقييمات)
// =====================
model CourierRating {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  courierId String   @map("courier_id")
  orderId   String   @unique @map("order_id")
  rating    Int
  comment   String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  courier   Courier  @relation(fields: [courierId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("courier_ratings")
}

# Routes

     1→---
     2→name: workflows
     3→description: Manage application workflows including configuration, restart, and removal.
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Overview
     7→
     8→A workflow binds a shell command (e.g., `npm run dev`, `python run.py`, `cargo run`) to a long-running task managed by Replit. Workflows are used to run webservers, background services, TUIs, and other persistent processes.
     9→
    10→**Key characteristics:**
    11→
    12→- Workflows run until explicitly stopped
    13→- The system tracks workflows automatically — no separate configuration file is required
    14→- Workflows auto-restart after package installation and module installation
    15→- You can only get console logs from a workflow if it is running
    16→
    17→## Setup Tips
    18→
    19→1. **Keep workflows minimal**: One workflow per project is usually sufficient. Use the main frontend server or TUI as your primary workflow.
    20→
    21→2. **Choose the right workflow**: If your project has a frontend or TUI, set the workflow to the process that updates what the user sees.
    22→
    23→3. **Clean up unused workflows**: When adding new workflows, remove any existing workflows that are no longer needed.
    24→
    25→4. **Always restart after changes**: Restart workflows after making server-side code changes to ensure updates are visible to the user. Verify they run without errors before returning to the user.
    26→
    27→5. **Use bash for one-off commands**: Workflows are for persistent processes. Use bash for build scripts, testing, or commands that don't need to keep running.
    28→
    29→## When to Use
    30→
    31→Use this skill when:
    32→
    33→- You need to create or configure a workflow (background process)
    34→- You need to restart the application after making code changes
    35→- The application needs to be restarted to pick up new environment variables
    36→- You need to remove a workflow that's no longer needed
    37→- The user asks to start, stop, or restart the application
    38→- You need to check what workflows are configured
    39→- You need to check workflow status, output, or open ports
    40→
    41→## When NOT to Use
    42→
    43→- For debugging runtime errors (fix the code first, then restart)
    44→- When the application is already running and changes are hot-reloaded
    45→- One-off commands (use bash for commands that don't need to persist)
    46→- Build scripts (use bash for npm build, webpack, etc.)
    47→- Testing commands (use bash or runTest callback)
    48→- More than 10 workflows (keep workflows minimal; combine services if needed)
    49→
    50→## Available Functions
    51→
    52→### listWorkflows()
    53→
    54→List all configured workflows with their current state.
    55→
    56→**Parameters:** None
    57→
    58→**Returns:** List of workflow info dicts with `name` (str), `command` (str), and `state` ("not_started", "running", "finished", "failed").
    59→
    60→**Example:**
    61→
    62→```javascript
    63→const workflows = await listWorkflows();
    64→for (const w of workflows) {
    65→    console.log(`${w.name}: ${w.state}`);
    66→}
    67→```
    68→
    69→### getWorkflowStatus(name, maxScrollbackLines)
    70→
    71→Get detailed status of a specific workflow including output logs and open ports.
    72→
    73→**Parameters:**
    74→
    75→- `name` (str, required): Name of the workflow to check
    76→- `maxScrollbackLines` (int, default 100): Number of output lines to include
    77→
    78→**Returns:** Dict with `name`, `command`, `state`, `output` (recent terminal output), `openPorts` (list of listening ports), and `waitForPort`.
    79→
    80→**Example:**
    81→
    82→```javascript
    83→// Check if the server is running and see its output
    84→const status = await getWorkflowStatus({ name: "Start application" });
    85→console.log(`State: ${status.state}`);
    86→if (status.output) {
    87→    console.log(`Output:\n${status.output}`);
    88→}
    89→if (status.openPorts) {
    90→    console.log(`Listening on ports: ${status.openPorts}`);
    91→}
    92→```
    93→
    94→### configureWorkflow({ name, command, waitForPort, outputType, autoStart, isCanvasWorkflow })
    95→
    96→Configure or create a workflow. This is the primary way to set up background processes.
    97→
    98→**Parameters:**
    99→
   100→- `name` (str, default "Start application"): Unique workflow identifier
   101→- `command` (str, required): Shell command to execute
   102→- `waitForPort` (int, optional): Port the process listens on
   103→- `outputType` (str, default "webview"): "webview", "console", or "vnc"
   104→- `autoStart` (bool, default True): Auto-start after configuration
   105→- `isCanvasWorkflow` (bool, default false): Whether this workflow serves canvas iframe content
   106→
   107→**Output Type Rules:**
   108→
   109→- **webview** - For web applications. MUST use port 5000.
   110→- **console** - For backend services, CLIs, TUIs. Can use any supported port.
   111→- **vnc** - For desktop/GUI apps (Electron, PyQt, etc.). No port needed.
   112→
   113→**Supported Ports:** 3000, 3001, 3002, 3003, 4200, 5000 (webview only), 5173, 6000, 6800, 8000, 8008, 8080, 8099, 9000
   114→
   115→**Examples:**
   116→
   117→```javascript
   118→// Web application (React, Next.js, etc.)
   119→await configureWorkflow({
   120→    name: "Start application",
   121→    command: "npm run dev",
   122→    waitForPort: 5000,
   123→    outputType: "webview"
   124→});
   125→
   126→// Backend API
   127→await configureWorkflow({
   128→    name: "Backend API",
   129→    command: "python api.py",
   130→    waitForPort: 8000,
   131→    outputType: "console"
   132→});
   133→
   134→// Desktop application
   135→await configureWorkflow({
   136→    name: "Desktop App",
   137→    command: "python gui_app.py",
   138→    outputType: "vnc"
   139→});
   140→```
   141→
   142→
   143→### removeWorkflow(name)
   144→
   145→Remove a workflow by name. Automatically stops it if running.
   146→
   147→**Parameters:**
   148→
   149→- `name` (str, required): Name of the workflow to remove
   150→
   151→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `workflowName`, and `wasRunning`
   152→
   153→**Example:**
   154→
   155→```javascript
   156→await removeWorkflow({ name: "Backend API" });
   157→```
   158→
   159→### restartWorkflow(workflowName, timeout)
   160→
   161→Restart a workflow by name.
   162→
   163→**Parameters:**
   164→
   165→- `workflowName` (str, required): Name of the workflow (e.g., "Start application")
   166→- `timeout` (int, default 30): Timeout in seconds to wait for restart
   167→
   168→**Returns:** Dict with restart status and optional screenshot URL
   169→
   170→**Example:**
   171→
   172→```javascript
   173→// Restart the main application
   174→const result = await restartWorkflow({ workflowName: "Start application" });
   175→console.log(result.message);
   176→
   177→// Restart with custom timeout
   178→const result2 = await restartWorkflow({
   179→    workflowName: "Start application",
   180→    timeout: 60
   181→});
   182→```
   183→
   184→## Best Practices
   185→
   186→1. **Restart after code changes**: Always restart workflows after modifying server-side code
   187→2. **Use appropriate timeouts**: Increase timeout for applications with slow startup
   188→3. **Check logs on failure**: If restart fails, check workflow logs for error details
   189→4. **One restart at a time**: Avoid parallel restarts of the same workflow
   190→
   191→5. **Port 5000 for web apps**: Always use port 5000 with webview output type
   192→
   193→
   194→## Common Workflow Names
   195→
   196→- `Start application` - Main application workflow
   197→- `Start Backend` - Backend server (in Expo/mobile apps)
   198→- `Project` - Parent workflow for multi-service apps
   199→
   200→## Error Handling
   201→
   202→The workflow functions may raise errors in these cases:
   203→
   204→- **Workflow not found**: The specified workflow name doesn't exist
   205→- **Port not opened**: The application didn't start listening on expected port
   206→- **Preview not available**: The application endpoint isn't responding with HTTP 200
   207→- **Workflow limit exceeded**: Maximum of 10 workflows reached
   208→
   209→- **Port/output type mismatch**: Port 5000 requires webview, webview requires port 5000
   210→
   211→
   212→When errors occur, check:
   213→
   214→1. Workflow logs for error details
   215→2. Application code for startup issues
   216→3. Port configuration in the workflow settings
   217→
   218→## Example Workflow
   219→
   220→```javascript
   221→// Create a web application workflow
   222→await configureWorkflow({
   223→    name: "Start application",
   224→    command: "npm run dev",
   225→
   226→    waitForPort: 5000,
   227→
   228→    outputType: "webview"
   229→});
   230→
   231→// After making code changes, restart the application
   232→const result = await restartWorkflow({ workflowName: "Start application" });
   233→
   234→if (result.success) {
   235→    console.log("Application restarted successfully");
   236→    if (result.screenshotUrl) {
   237→        console.log(`Screenshot: ${result.screenshotUrl}`);
   238→    }
   239→} else {
   240→    console.log(`Restart failed: ${result.message}`);
   241→}
   242→
   243→// Clean up unused workflows
   244→await removeWorkflow({ name: "Old Backend" });
   245→```

     1→---
     2→name: package-management
     3→description: Install and manage language packages, system dependencies, and programming language runtimes.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Package Management Skill
     7→
     8→Manage project dependencies and programming language runtimes. Use this skill instead of running shell commands like `npm install`, `pip install`, or `apt install`.
     9→
    10→## When to Use
    11→
    12→Use this skill when you need to:
    13→
    14→- Install language-specific packages (npm, pip, cargo, etc.)
    15→- Install system-level dependencies (ffmpeg, jq, imagemagick, etc.)
    16→- Install programming language runtimes (Python, Node.js, etc.)
    17→- Remove packages from the project
    18→- Check available versions of a programming language
    19→
    20→## When NOT to Use
    21→
    22→- Searching for available packages (use web search instead)
    23→- Configuring package settings (edit config files directly)
    24→- Running package scripts (use bash tool)
    25→
    26→## Modules (Replit Terminology)
    27→
    28→"Modules" is a Replit-specific term for language toolchains that can be installed into the NixOS environment. Use `listAvailableModules()` to see what's available.
    29→
    30→**Installation priority order:**
    31→
    32→1. Modules via this skill (preferred)
    33→2. Nix system dependencies via `installSystemDependencies()`
    34→3. Language package managers (pip, npm, cargo, etc.) via `installLanguagePackages()`
    35→
    36→If confused about package installation in Nix or language package managers, use web search.
    37→
    38→If a package install fails with HTTP 403 from `package-firewall.replit.local`, treat it as a likely security block and do not retry the same install.
    39→
    40→**After installing a module:**
    41→
    42→- Update `.gitignore` with the language's standard ignore patterns
    43→- Never add Replit config files to `.gitignore`
    44→
    45→**After removing a module:**
    46→
    47→- Consider removing any corresponding workflows that depend on it
    48→
    49→## Available Functions
    50→
    51→### listAvailableModules(langName=None)
    52→
    53→List available language toolchains that can be installed.
    54→
    55→**Parameters:**
    56→
    57→- `langName` (str, optional): Language name to filter by (e.g., "python", "nodejs", "rust"). If not provided, returns all available modules.
    58→
    59→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `langName`, and `modules` list
    60→
    61→Each module contains: id, name, version, description
    62→
    63→**Example:**
    64→
    65→```javascript
    66→// List all available modules
    67→const modules = await listAvailableModules();
    68→// Returns: {modules: [{id: "python-3.11", ...}, {id: "nodejs-20", ...}, ...]}
    69→
    70→// Find available Python versions
    71→const pythonModules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
    72→// Returns: {modules: [{id: "python-3.11", name: "Python", version: "3.11", ...}, ...]}
    73→
    74→// Find available Node.js versions
    75→const nodeModules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "nodejs" });
    76→```
    77→
    78→### installProgrammingLanguage(language)
    79→
    80→Install a programming language runtime and its package manager.
    81→
    82→**Parameters:**
    83→
    84→- `language` (str, required): Language identifier like "python-3.11", "nodejs-20"
    85→
    86→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `language`, and `installedModuleId` keys
    87→
    88→**Example:**
    89→
    90→```javascript
    91→// First, check available versions
    92→const modules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
    93→console.log(modules);  // See available Python versions
    94→
    95→// Install specific version
    96→const result = await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "python-3.11" });
    97→
    98→// Install Node.js 20
    99→const result2 = await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "nodejs-20" });
   100→```
   101→
   102→### uninstallProgrammingLanguage(moduleId)
   103→
   104→Remove an installed programming language runtime.
   105→
   106→**Parameters:**
   107→
   108→- `moduleId` (str, required): Module ID from `listAvailableModules`
   109→
   110→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `moduleId`, and `wasInstalled`
   111→
   112→**Example:**
   113→
   114→```javascript
   115→// Remove Python 3.10
   116→const result = await uninstallProgrammingLanguage({ moduleId: "python-3.10" });
   117→```
   118→
   119→### installLanguagePackages(language, packages)
   120→
   121→Install language-specific packages like npm, pip, or cargo packages.
   122→
   123→**Parameters:**
   124→
   125→- `language` (str, required): Programming language: "nodejs", "python", "bun", "go", "rust"
   126→- `packages` (list[str], required): List of packages to install
   127→
   128→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `packages`, and `output` keys
   129→
   130→**Example:**
   131→
   132→```javascript
   133→// Install npm packages
   134→const result = await installLanguagePackages({
   135→    language: "nodejs",
   136→    packages: ["express", "lodash"]
   137→});
   138→console.log(result.message);
   139→
   140→// Install pip packages
   141→const result2 = await installLanguagePackages({
   142→    language: "python",
   143→    packages: ["requests", "flask"]
   144→});
   145→```
   146→
   147→**IMPORTANT — required parameter rules:**
   148→
   149→- `language` is **required** — you must always include it
   150→- Valid language values: `"nodejs"`, `"python"`, `"bun"`, `"go"`, `"rust"`
   151→  - Use `"nodejs"` for JavaScript/TypeScript projects — NEVER use `"js"`, `"node"`, or `"javascript"`
   152→  - Use `"python"` for Python projects — NEVER use `"py"` or `"pip"`
   153→- `packages` must be an **array of strings** — NEVER a single string, NEVER an array of objects
   154→  - Correct: `packages: ["express"]`
   155→  - Wrong: `packages: "express"`
   156→  - Wrong: `packages: [{name: "express"}]`
   157→
   158→### uninstallLanguagePackages(language, packages)
   159→
   160→Remove language-specific packages.
   161→
   162→**Parameters:**
   163→
   164→- `language` (str, required): Programming language
   165→- `packages` (list[str], required): List of packages to uninstall
   166→
   167→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   168→
   169→**Example:**
   170→
   171→```javascript
   172→const result = await uninstallLanguagePackages({
   173→    language: "nodejs",
   174→    packages: ["lodash"]
   175→});
   176→```
   177→
   178→### installSystemDependencies(packages)
   179→
   180→Install system-level dependencies via Nix.
   181→
   182→**Parameters:**
   183→
   184→- `packages` (list[str], required): Nixpkgs attribute paths (NOT apt package names)
   185→
   186→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   187→
   188→**Important:** Use Nix package names, not apt/debian names:
   189→
   190→- X11 libraries need 'xorg.' prefix: `xorg.libxcb`, `xorg.libX11`
   191→- `ca-certificates` is `cacert` in Nix
   192→- `libxcb` is `xorg.libxcb` in Nix
   193→
   194→**Example:**
   195→
   196→```javascript
   197→// Install system dependencies
   198→const result = await installSystemDependencies({
   199→    packages: ["jq", "ffmpeg", "imagemagick"]
   200→});
   201→
   202→// Install X11 libraries (note the xorg. prefix)
   203→const result2 = await installSystemDependencies({
   204→    packages: ["xorg.libxcb", "xorg.libX11"]
   205→});
   206→```
   207→
   208→### uninstallSystemDependencies(packages)
   209→
   210→Remove system-level dependencies.
   211→
   212→**Parameters:**
   213→
   214→- `packages` (list[str], required): Nixpkgs attribute paths to uninstall
   215→
   216→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   217→
   218→**Example:**
   219→
   220→```javascript
   221→const result = await uninstallSystemDependencies({ packages: ["jq"] });
   222→```
   223→
   224→## Best Practices
   225→
   226→1. **Use language packages for project dependencies**: These are tracked in package files (package.json, requirements.txt)
   227→2. **Use system dependencies for OS-level tools**: Things like ffmpeg, imagemagick, or native libraries
   228→3. **Use Nix package names**: Not apt/debian names. Check nixpkgs for correct names
   229→4. **Install language runtimes when needed**: If `python` or `node` commands fail, install the runtime first
   230→5. **Check versions first**: Use `listAvailableModules` before `installProgrammingLanguage`
   231→
   232→## Example Workflow
   233→
   234→```javascript
   235→// Check available Python versions
   236→const modules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
   237→console.log(modules);
   238→
   239→// Set up a Python Flask project
   240→await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "python-3.11" });
   241→await installLanguagePackages({
   242→    language: "python",
   243→    packages: ["flask", "gunicorn", "sqlalchemy"]
   244→});
   245→
   246→// Set up a Node.js project with native dependencies
   247→await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "nodejs-20" });
   248→await installLanguagePackages({
   249→    language: "nodejs",
   250→    packages: ["sharp", "canvas"]
   251→});
   252→await installSystemDependencies({
   253→    packages: ["pkg-config", "cairo", "pango", "libjpeg"]
   254→});
   255→
   256→// Clean up old language version
   257→await uninstallProgrammingLanguage({ moduleId: "python-3.10" });
   258→```
   259→
   260→## Automatic Behaviors
   261→
   262→- Installing packages automatically creates/updates project files (package.json, requirements.txt, etc.)
   263→- Package installations reboot all workflows to pick up new dependencies
   264→- Language runtime installations include popular package managers (pip, npm, etc.)

# مندوبك - Mandoubak Delivery Platform

## نظرة عامة
منصة دليفري كاملة زي أوبر. 3 أنواع يوزر: عميل + مندوب + أدمن.

## التكنولوجيا
- **Frontend**: React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- **Backend**: Hono.js (Cloudflare Workers-compatible) + Node.js local server
- **Auth**: Clerk (Google/Phone/OTP)
- **Database**: Supabase PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM
- **Cache/Lock**: Cloudflare KV (Race Condition prevention)
- **Storage**: Cloudflare R2 (ID card images)
- **Realtime**: Supabase Realtime

## هيكل المشروع
```
mandoubak/
├── frontend/          # React + Vite app (port 5000)
├── backend/           # Hono.js API (port 8787)
├── prisma/            # Database schema
└── sql/               # RLS Policies for Supabase
```

## تشغيل محلياً
```bash
cd frontend && npm run dev
```

## الـ API Endpoints
- `GET  /api/pricing/calculate` - حساب سعر التوصيل
- `POST /api/orders` - إنشاء أوردر
- `GET  /api/orders/pending` - الأوردرات المنتظرة (للمناديب)
- `POST /api/orders/:id/accept` - قبول أوردر
- `POST /api/couriers/register` - تسجيل مندوب
- `POST /api/upload-id` - رفع صور البطاقة

## الـ Setup للإنتاج
1. انسخ `.env.example` لـ `.env` وحط فيه القيم
2. Deploy الـ backend على Cloudflare Workers
3. Deploy الـ frontend على Cloudflare Pages
4. شغّل `prisma migrate` على Supabase

import React from 'react'

import { Toaster } from 'react-hot-toast'
import App from './App'
import { AuthProvider } from './lib/auth-context'
import { NotificationsProvider } from './lib/notifications-context'
import './styles/globals.css'

              style: {
                fontFamily: 'Cairo, sans-serif',

import { Routes, Route, Navigate } from 'react-router-dom'
import Layout from './components/Layout'
import HomePage from './pages/HomePage'
import OrderPage from './pages/OrderPage'
import MyOrders from './pages/MyOrders'
import TrackOrder from './pages/TrackOrder'
import Notifications from './pages/Notifications'
import LoginPage from './pages/auth/Login'
import CourierDashboard from './pages/courier/Dashboard'
import CourierProfile from './pages/courier/Profile'
import CourierRegister from './pages/courier/Register'
import AdminDashboard from './pages/admin/Dashboard'
import AdminPricing from './pages/admin/Pricing'
import AdminAds from './pages/admin/Ads'
import AdminCouriers from './pages/admin/Couriers'

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Routes>
      <Route element={<Layout />}>
        {/* Public */}
        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
        <Route path="/login" element={<LoginPage />} />
        <Route path="/notifications" element={<Notifications />} />

        {/* Client */}
        <Route path="/order" element={<OrderPage />} />
        <Route path="/my-orders" element={<MyOrders />} />
        <Route path="/track/:id" element={<TrackOrder />} />

        {/* Courier */}
        <Route path="/courier/register" element={<CourierRegister />} />
        <Route path="/courier/dashboard" element={<CourierDashboard />} />
        <Route path="/courier/profile" element={<CourierProfile />} />

        {/* Admin */}
        <Route path="/admin" element={<AdminDashboard />} />
        <Route path="/admin/pricing" element={<AdminPricing />} />
        <Route path="/admin/ads" element={<AdminAds />} />
        <Route path="/admin/couriers" element={<AdminCouriers />} />
      </Route>
      <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />
    </Routes>
  )
}

---
name: Mandoubak Project State
description: Full state of the Mandoubak delivery platform — what's built, architecture, and key decisions
---

# All Pages Complete

## Client pages
- `/` — HomePage with ads grid, hero, stats
- `/order` — 3-step order flow (type → addresses → confirm + price calc)
- `/my-orders` — order list with filter tabs + rating modal
- `/track/:id` — animated fake map + live order progress
- `/notifications` — notification list + unread count
- `/profile` — client profile (view/edit + stats)

## Courier pages
- `/courier/register` — 3-step registration (info → ID upload → review)
- `/courier/dashboard` — live order feed + accept with race condition simulation
- `/courier/profile` — stats + ratings card
- `/courier/earnings` — earnings dashboard with period selector (day/week/month/all)

## Admin pages
- `/admin` — overview stats dashboard
- `/admin/pricing` — pricing calculator config
- `/admin/ads` — manage restaurant/vendor ads
- `/admin/couriers` — approve/reject courier applications

## Auth
- `/login` — Demo Mode role selector (client/courier/admin)
- Pure Demo Mode — no Clerk keys needed, stores role in localStorage
- `lib/auth-context.tsx` — AuthProvider + useAuth hook
- `lib/notifications-context.tsx` — NotificationsProvider + useNotifications

## Layout
- Navbar: logo + desktop nav links + notification bell badge + user dropdown
- BottomNav: mobile-only, role-aware (different tabs per role)
- Both in `components/Layout.tsx` and `components/BottomNav.tsx`

## Key decisions
**Why Demo Mode:** Clerk requires real API keys, removed all Clerk imports to allow the app to run without setup. Auth state stored in localStorage.
**Why role-aware BottomNav:** Each user type (client/courier/admin/guest) gets different nav items optimized for their workflow.

   231→-- =============================================================
   232→-- Function: قبول الأوردر بـ Atomic Transaction (منع Race Condition)
   233→-- =============================================================
   234→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION accept_order(
   235→  p_order_id TEXT,
   236→  p_courier_id TEXT
   237→)
   238→RETURNS JSON AS $$
   239→DECLARE
   240→  v_order orders%ROWTYPE;
   241→  v_result JSON;
   242→BEGIN
   243→  -- SELECT FOR UPDATE يقفل الصف عشان محدش يقدر ياخده في نفس الوقت
   244→  SELECT * INTO v_order
   245→  FROM orders
   246→  WHERE id = p_order_id
   247→  FOR UPDATE NOWAIT;
   248→
   249→  -- لو الأوردر مش pending، يبعت رسالة خطأ
   250→  IF v_order.status != 'PENDING' THEN
   251→    v_result := json_build_object(
   252→      'success', false,
   253→      'message', 'الأوردر اتحجز من مندوب تاني'
   254→    );
   255→    RETURN v_result;
   256→  END IF;
   257→
   258→  -- تحديث الأوردر لـ ACCEPTED
   259→  UPDATE orders
   260→  SET
   261→    status = 'ACCEPTED',
   262→    courier_id = p_courier_id,
   263→    accepted_at = NOW(),
   264→    updated_at = NOW()
   265→  WHERE id = p_order_id;
   266→
   267→  v_result := json_build_object(
   268→    'success', true,
   269→    'message', 'تم قبول الأوردر بنجاح',
   270→    'order_id', p_order_id,
   271→    'courier_id', p_courier_id
   272→  );
   273→
   274→  RETURN v_result;
   275→
   276→EXCEPTION
   277→  WHEN lock_not_available THEN
   278→    v_result := json_build_object(
   279→      'success', false,
   280→      'message', 'الأوردر بيتحجز دلوقتي، جرب تاني'
   281→    );
   282→    RETURN v_result;
   283→END;
   284→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

     1→// =============================================================
     2→// Auth Middleware - Clerk Token Verification
     3→// =============================================================
     4→
     5→import { createMiddleware } from 'hono/factory'
     6→import { createClerkClient } from '@clerk/backend'
     7→import type { Env } from '../index'
     8→
     9→export type AuthUser = {
    10→  userId: string
    11→  clerkId: string
    12→  role: 'CLIENT' | 'COURIER' | 'ADMIN'
    13→  phone?: string
    14→}
    15→
    16→declare module 'hono' {
    17→  interface ContextVariableMap {
    18→    user: AuthUser
    19→  }
    20→}
    21→
    22→export const authMiddleware = createMiddleware<{ Bindings: Env }>(async (c, next) => {
    23→  const authHeader = c.req.header('Authorization')
    24→  
    25→  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
    26→    return c.json({ error: 'مش مسجل دخول' }, 401)
    27→  }
    28→
    29→  const token = authHeader.split(' ')[1]
    30→
    31→  try {
    32→    const clerk = createClerkClient({ secretKey: c.env.CLERK_SECRET_KEY })
    33→    const payload = await clerk.verifyToken(token)
    34→    
    35→    // جيب role اليوزر من Supabase
    36→    const { createClient } = await import('@supabase/supabase-js')
    37→    const supabase = createClient(c.env.SUPABASE_URL, c.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)
    38→    
    39→    const { data: user } = await supabase
    40→      .from('users')
    41→      .select('id, role, phone')
    42→      .eq('clerk_id', payload.sub)
    43→      .single()
    44→
    45→    if (!user) {
    46→      return c.json({ error: 'اليوزر مش موجود' }, 401)
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    c.set('user', {
    50→      userId: user.id,
    51→      clerkId: payload.sub,
    52→      role: user.role,
    53→      phone: user.phone,
    54→    })
    55→
    56→    await next()
    57→  } catch (err) {
    58→    return c.json({ error: 'التوكن مش صحيح' }, 401)
    59→  }
    60→})
    61→
    62→export const requireRole = (...roles: AuthUser['role'][]) => 
    63→  createMiddleware<{ Bindings: Env }>(async (c, next) => {
    64→    const user = c.get('user')
    65→    if (!roles.includes(user.role)) {
    66→      return c.json({ error: 'مش عندك صلاحية' }, 403)
    67→    }
    68→    await next()
    69→  })

   192→ordersRouter.post('/:id/accept', requireRole('COURIER'), async (c) => {
   193→  const user = c.get('user')
   194→  const orderId = c.req.param('id')
   195→
   196→  const supabase = createClient(c.env.SUPABASE_URL, c.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)
   197→
   198→  // جيب الـ courier_id من الـ database
   199→  const { data: courier } = await supabase
   200→    .from('couriers')
   201→    .select('id, status')
   202→    .eq('user_id', user.userId)
   203→    .single()
   204→
   205→  if (!courier) {
   206→    return c.json({ error: 'بروفايل المندوب مش موجود' }, 400)
   207→  }
   208→
   209→  if (courier.status !== 'APPROVED') {
   210→    return c.json({ error: 'حسابك لسه تحت المراجعة' }, 403)
   211→  }
   212→
   213→  // Step 1: KV Lock (منع السباق على مستوى الـ Edge)
   214→  const lockResult = await acquireOrderLock(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV, orderId, courier.id)
   215→
   216→  if (!lockResult.acquired) {
   217→    return c.json({
   218→      success: false,
   219→      error: 'الأوردر اتحجز',
   220→      message: lockResult.reason
   221→    }, 409)
   222→  }
   223→
   224→  try {
   225→    // Step 2: Atomic Transaction في Supabase (منع السباق على مستوى الـ DB)
   226→    const { data: result, error } = await supabase
   227→      .rpc('accept_order', {
   228→        p_order_id: orderId,
   229→        p_courier_id: courier.id
   230→      })
   231→
   232→    if (error) {
   233→      await releaseOrderLock(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV, orderId)
   234→      return c.json({ error: 'خطأ في قبول الأوردر' }, 500)
   235→    }
   236→
   237→    if (!result.success) {
   238→      await releaseOrderLock(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV, orderId)
   239→      return c.json({
   240→        success: false,
   241→        error: result.message
   242→      }, 409)
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    // Step 3: مسح الـ Cache فوراً عشان الأوردر يختفي من المناديب
   246→    await invalidatePendingOrdersCache(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV)
   247→
   248→    // Step 4: فك الـ lock بعد ما التحديث اتعمل
   249→    await releaseOrderLock(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV, orderId)
   250→
   251→    return c.json({
   252→      success: true,
   253→      message: 'تم قبول الأوردر بنجاح!',
   254→      orderId,
   255→    })
   256→
   257→  } catch (err) {
   258→    await releaseOrderLock(c.env.MANDOUBAK_KV, orderId)
   259→    return c.json({ error: 'خطأ غير متوقع' }, 500)
   260→  }
   261→})

     1→// =============================================================
     2→// Cloudflare KV Lock - منع Race Condition على الأوردرات
     3→// =============================================================
     4→
     5→export type KVLockResult = 
     6→  | { acquired: true; lockKey: string }
     7→  | { acquired: false; reason: string }
     8→
     9→const LOCK_TTL_SECONDS = 10  // 10 ثواني timeout للـ lock
    10→const CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 3  // 3 ثواني cache للأوردرات الجديدة
    11→
    12→/**
    13→ * يحاول يحجز lock على أوردر معين
    14→ * بيستخدم KV atomic write عشان يضمن مندوب واحد بس ياخد الأوردر
    15→ */
    16→export async function acquireOrderLock(
    17→  kv: KVNamespace,
    18→  orderId: string,
    19→  courierId: string
    20→): Promise<KVLockResult> {
    21→  const lockKey = `order_lock:${orderId}`
    22→  
    23→  // محاولة كتابة الـ lock
    24→  // لو فيه value موجود، الـ KV put بياخد المكان
    25→  // بنستخدم خطوتين: GET ثم PUT داخل نفس الـ request
    26→  
    27→  const existing = await kv.get(lockKey)
    28→  
    29→  if (existing !== null) {
    30→    return {
    31→      acquired: false,
    32→      reason: `الأوردر محجوز من مندوب تاني (${existing})`
    33→    }
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  // ضع الـ lock مع TTL
    37→  await kv.put(lockKey, courierId, { expirationTtl: LOCK_TTL_SECONDS })
    38→  
    39→  // تحقق إن اللي كتبه هو نفسه (Double-check بعد الكتابة)
    40→  const verify = await kv.get(lockKey)
    41→  
    42→  if (verify !== courierId) {
    43→    return {
    44→      acquired: false,
    45→      reason: 'مندوب تاني سبقك في نفس اللحظة'
    46→    }
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  return { acquired: true, lockKey }
    50→}
    51→
    52→/**
    53→ * يفك الـ lock بعد ما الأوردر يتحجز في الـ database
    54→ */
    55→export async function releaseOrderLock(
    56→  kv: KVNamespace,
    57→  orderId: string
    58→): Promise<void> {

    66→ */
    67→export async function cachePendingOrders(

    81→export async function getCachedPendingOrders(

---
name: Mandoubak Project State
description: Full state of the Mandoubak delivery platform — what's built, architecture, and key decisions
---

# All Pages Complete

## Client pages
- `/` — HomePage with ads grid, hero, stats
- `/order` — 3-step order flow (type → addresses → confirm + price calc)
- `/my-orders` — order list with filter tabs + rating modal
- `/track/:id` — animated fake map + live order progress
- `/notifications` — notification list + unread count
- `/profile` — client profile (view/edit + stats)

## Courier pages
- `/courier/register` — 3-step registration (info → ID upload → review)
- `/courier/dashboard` — live order feed + accept with race condition simulation
- `/courier/profile` — stats + ratings card
- `/courier/earnings` — earnings dashboard with period selector (day/week/month/all)

## Admin pages
- `/admin` — overview stats dashboard
- `/admin/pricing` — pricing calculator config
- `/admin/ads` — manage restaurant/vendor ads
- `/admin/couriers` — approve/reject courier applications

## Auth
- `/login` — Demo Mode role selector (client/courier/admin)
- Pure Demo Mode — no Clerk keys needed, stores role in localStorage
- `lib/auth-context.tsx` — AuthProvider + useAuth hook
- `lib/notifications-context.tsx` — NotificationsProvider + useNotifications

## Layout
- Navbar: logo + desktop nav links + notification bell badge + user dropdown
- BottomNav: mobile-only, role-aware (different tabs per role)
- Both in `components/Layout.tsx` and `components/BottomNav.tsx`

# Backend (Hono.js + Cloudflare Workers)

## Routes
- `POST /api/orders` — create order (CLIENT only, validated)
- `GET /api/orders/pending` — pending orders (COURIER/ADMIN only, KV cached 3s)
- `POST /api/orders/:id/accept` — accept order (KV lock + DB SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT)
- `GET /api/orders/my` — user's own orders
- `PATCH /api/orders/:id/status` — update status (courier ownership checked)
- `POST /api/couriers/register` — register courier
- `GET /api/couriers/profile` — courier profile
- `PATCH /api/couriers/online-status` — toggle availability
- `POST /api/couriers/rate/:orderId` — rate a courier
- `GET/POST /api/admin/pricing` — pricing management (ADMIN only)
- `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/ads` — ads management (ADMIN only, strict validation)
- `GET /api/admin/couriers` — list couriers
- `PATCH /api/admin/couriers/:id/approve` — approve/reject
- `GET /api/admin/orders` — all orders with pagination
- `POST /api/upload/id` — upload ID card images (R2)
- `POST /api/upload/product` — upload product image (ADMIN only)
- `GET /api/upload/view` — serve image with signed URL + path traversal protection
- `GET /api/upload/signed/:key` — generate signed URL

## Security fixes applied
- CORS: dynamic origin check, supports Replit domains
- secureHeaders middleware added
- All JSON bodies wrapped in try/catch
- Admin ads PATCH uses `.strict()` schema + explicit field mapping (no raw spread)
- Order status PATCH verifies courier owns the order
- Upload routes: path traversal prevention, ADMIN-only product upload, ownership check
- Input validation on all IDs (length check)
- Pickup ≠ delivery validation
- Items capped at 50 per order
- Pagination page capped at 1000

## Database (Supabase PostgreSQL)
- RLS on all 9 tables
- `accept_order()` function with SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT (atomic)
- REVOKE/GRANT on accept_order — service_role only
- Trigger: `update_courier_avg_rating` auto-updates courier.rating
- Performance indexes: orders(status), orders(client_id), orders(courier_id), couriers(status), ratings(courier_id), ads(is_active, end_date)
- User self-role-escalation prevented in RLS

## Key decisions
**Why Demo Mode:** Clerk requires real API keys. App runs without setup in Demo Mode.
**Why role-aware BottomNav:** Each user type gets optimized tab set.
**Why TypeScript `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>[]`:** `NodeJS.Timeout` not available without @types/node — tsconfig only includes vite/client types.
**Why KV lock + DB lock:** KV is edge-layer (fast), DB SELECT FOR UPDATE is the true atomic guard. Two layers = maximum safety.
